description: Two initiatives: (1) Standardize ingredient editing across the app — replace the choppy EditRecipeIngredientsDialog/IngredientFormRows with inline GroceryItemRow-style editing (RecipeIngredientList component) and a shared AI-parse AddIngredientInput, used consistently in RecipeCard, EventRecipesTab, and per-recipe grocery tabs. (2) Extract the Recipes/Groceries/Pantry tab shell into a shared RecipeDetailTabs component and wire it into PersonalMealDetailPage, which currently has no Groceries or Pantry tabs.

userStories.title: Extract parseIngredientText shared utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared utility function for AI-parsing freeform ingredient text so that both the grocery hook and the standalone RecipeIngredientList component can call it without duplicating the temp-recipe pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at src/lib/parseIngredientText.ts
Exports parseIngredientText(text: string, userId: string): Promise<ParsedGroceryItem[]>

Implementation is the temp-recipe create → supabase.functions.invoke('parse-recipe') → cleanup pattern, extracted verbatim from useGroceryList.handleBulkParseGroceryText (lines ~598-632 of src/hooks/useGroceryList.ts)
ParsedGroceryItem is imported from @/components/recipes/GroceryListSection (already exported there)
useGroceryList.handleBulkParseGroceryText now calls parseIngredientText(text, userId) instead of duplicating the logic
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The exact code to extract is in src/hooks/useGroceryList.ts lines ~598-632 (handleBulkParseGroceryText callback). It: (1) inserts a temp recipe row into 'recipes' table with name='General Items', (2) calls supabase.functions.invoke('parse-recipe', { body: { recipeId: tempRecipe.id, recipeName: 'General Items', text } }), (3) deletes the temp recipe, (4) returns data.parsed?.ingredients. The function needs supabase client — import from @/integrations/supabase/client. ParsedGroceryItem type is exported from src/components/recipes/GroceryListSection.tsx (line 16-21). After extracting, update handleBulkParseGroceryText to be a thin wrapper: return parseIngredientText(text, userId).

userStories.title: Create AddIngredientInput component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable textarea+button component for adding ingredients via AI text parsing so that the same UI is used in the General grocery tab, per-recipe grocery tabs, and RecipeIngredientList.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at src/components/recipes/AddIngredientInput.tsx
Props: onSubmit: (text: string) => Promise<void>, placeholder?: string, className?: string
Renders a Textarea and an 'Add' button (same visual pattern as the General tab in GroceryListSection, lines ~400-429)
Shows Loader2 spinner on the button while onSubmit is in flight
Button and textarea are disabled while submitting
Clears textarea after successful submit
Does nothing if textarea is empty
GroceryListSection General tab (lines ~399-429) is updated to use AddIngredientInput instead of its current inline textarea+button, passing handleBulkAdd as onSubmit
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The exact UI to extract is in src/components/recipes/GroceryListSection.tsx lines ~399-429 (the 'Add general items' section). It has a Textarea with placeholder 'Add ingredients, e.g. 2 cups flour, 1 lb chicken, olive oil', a flex justify-end div, and a Button that calls handleBulkAdd. Extract these into AddIngredientInput. The loading state (isParsing) is currently managed by GroceryListSection — in the new component, manage it internally with useState. The onSubmit prop receives the text string; the component handles showing loading and clearing the textarea. Import Textarea from @/components/ui/textarea, Button from @/components/ui/button, Loader2 and Plus from lucide-react.

userStories.title: Create RecipeIngredientList component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and inline-edit a recipe's ingredients using the same clean GroceryItemRow style as the grocery tab, so that there is one consistent ingredient editing experience across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at src/components/recipes/RecipeIngredientList.tsx
Props: recipeId: string, userId: string, editable?: boolean, onIngredientsChange?: () => void, cacheContext?: { type: 'event' | 'meal_plan', id: string, userId: string }
On mount, loads recipe ingredients via supabase.from('recipe_ingredients').select().eq('recipe_id', recipeId).order('sort_order')
Shows a loading spinner while fetching
Displays ingredients grouped by category using GroceryCategoryGroup (import from @/components/recipes/GroceryCategoryGroup)
Converts RecipeIngredient[] to SmartGroceryItem[] for GroceryCategoryGroup: { name, totalQuantity, unit, category, sourceRecipes: [] }
When editable=true, passes onEditItemText and onRemoveItem to GroceryCategoryGroup
Edit (onEditItemText): calls supabase.from('recipe_ingredients').update({ name: newText }).eq('id', matchedIngredient.id), then reloads ingredients and calls onIngredientsChange
Delete (onRemoveItem): calls supabase.from('recipe_ingredients').delete().eq('id', matchedIngredient.id), then reloads ingredients and calls onIngredientsChange
When editable=true, renders AddIngredientInput below the ingredient list

Add (AddIngredientInput onSubmit): calls parseIngredientText(text, userId), inserts each parsed item to recipe_ingredients via supabase.from('recipe_ingredients').insert([...]), then reloads, invalidates cacheContext if provided (via deleteGroceryCache), calls onIngredientsChange
Empty state shows 'No ingredients yet' when ingredient list is empty
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core new component. Key imports: GroceryCategoryGroup from @/components/recipes/GroceryCategoryGroup, AddIngredientInput from @/components/recipes/AddIngredientInput, parseIngredientText from @/lib/parseIngredientText, deleteGroceryCache from @/lib/groceryCache, supabase from @/integrations/supabase/client. The RecipeIngredient type is in src/types/index.ts. SmartGroceryItem conversion: map each RecipeIngredient to { name: ing.name, displayName: undefined, totalQuantity: ing.quantity ?? undefined, unit: ing.unit ?? undefined, category: ing.category, sourceRecipes: [] }. For grouping, use the groupSmartByCategory helper pattern from GroceryListSection (or CATEGORY_ORDER from @/lib/groceryList). Matching ingredients by name for edit/delete: find in the local ingredients state where ing.name.toLowerCase() === originalName.toLowerCase(). Insert parsed items: each ParsedGroceryItem becomes { recipe_id: recipeId, name: item.name, quantity: item.quantity, unit: item.unit, category: item.category, sort_order: existingIngredients.length + index }. deleteGroceryCache signature: deleteGroceryCache(type, id, userId) — import from src/lib/groceryCache.ts.

userStories.title: Add handleAddItemsToRecipe to useGroceryList
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a hook function that parses freeform text and adds the results to a specific recipe's ingredients so that per-recipe grocery tabs can offer the same Add Item experience.
acceptanceCriteria: useGroceryList hook exports handleAddItemsToRecipe: (recipeId: string, text: string) => Promise<void>
Calls parseIngredientText(text, userId) from @/lib/parseIngredientText
Inserts each parsed item to recipe_ingredients: { recipe_id: recipeId, name, quantity, unit, category, sort_order }
After insert, calls invalidateCacheAndResetRefs() and startRecombineTimer() (same pattern as other mutation handlers in the hook)
Also updates recipeIngredients state optimistically or triggers a reload so the per-recipe tab reflects the change immediately
handleAddItemsToRecipe is included in the hook's return value
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add this alongside the other handle* callbacks in src/hooks/useGroceryList.ts. The hook already has recipeIngredients state and invalidateCacheAndResetRefs/startRecombineTimer helpers. For sort_order: query existing count for this recipe first, or just use recipeIngredients.filter(r => r.recipeId === recipeId).length + index as a starting sort_order. The hook's return type interface is defined at the top of the file (lines ~50-80) — add handleAddItemsToRecipe to that interface. After insert, the recipeIngredients state should be updated to include the new items so the grocery tab updates without a full page reload: setRecipeIngredients(prev => [...prev, ...newItems]) where newItems are mapped to the RecipeIngredient shape.

userStories.title: Add AddIngredientInput to per-recipe grocery tabs
userStories.description: As a user on a per-recipe grocery tab, I want an 'Add ingredient' input at the bottom so that I can add missing ingredients using the same AI-parse flow as the General tab.
acceptanceCriteria: GroceryListSection has new prop: onAddItemsToRecipe?: (recipeId: string, text: string) => Promise<void>
AddIngredientInput appears at the bottom of each per-recipe TabsContent (not on Combined tab, not on General tab)
AddIngredientInput is not rendered when onAddItemsToRecipe is not provided
Clicking Add parses text and adds items to the recipe's ingredients, then the tab refreshes to show new items
MealPlanPage passes grocery.handleAddItemsToRecipe as onAddItemsToRecipe to GroceryListSection
EventDetailPage passes grocery.handleAddItemsToRecipe as onAddItemsToRecipe to GroceryListSection
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modify src/components/recipes/GroceryListSection.tsx. The per-recipe tab content is rendered in a .map() block starting around line 328. After the GroceryCategoryGroup blocks inside each TabsContent, add: {onAddItemsToRecipe && <AddIngredientInput onSubmit={(text) => onAddItemsToRecipe(recipe.id, text)} placeholder='Add ingredient, e.g. 2 tbsp olive oil' className='mt-3 border-t pt-3' />}. The Combined tab (TabsContent value='combined') should NOT have this — its content is lines ~296-326. The General tab already has AddIngredientInput from US-002. Wire up in MealPlanPage.tsx (grep for GroceryListSection usage around line 480-530) and EventDetailPage.tsx (grep for GroceryListSection around line 978-1007).

userStories.title: Replace EditRecipeIngredientsDialog in RecipeCard with inline RecipeIngredientList
userStories.description: As a user browsing My Recipes, I want to see and edit a recipe's ingredients inline on the recipe card — expanding with the same clean style as the grocery tab — instead of opening a separate choppy dialog.
acceptanceCriteria: RecipeCard's existing read-only ingredient section (lines ~273-309) is replaced with RecipeIngredientList
RecipeIngredientList is shown when the 'X ingredients' toggle is expanded (existing ingredientsExpanded state)
RecipeIngredientList receives editable={recipe.createdBy === userId} so only the recipe owner sees edit controls
RecipeCard accepts new props: userId?: string, onIngredientsChange?: () => void
The ListChecks button (onEditIngredients) is removed from RecipeCard action buttons
onEditIngredients prop is removed from RecipeCardProps
In RecipeHub.tsx: remove editIngredientsRecipe state, EditRecipeIngredientsDialog import and usage, and the onEditIngredients prop passed to RecipeCard
RecipeHub passes userId={user?.id} and onIngredientsChange callback (that reloads recipeIngredientsMap for the affected recipe) to RecipeCard
The 'X ingredients' count shown on the toggle button is now driven by RecipeIngredientList's internal state (or RecipeHub's recipeIngredientsMap — keep whichever is simpler)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: RecipeCard is at src/components/recipes/RecipeCard.tsx (~400 lines). The ingredient section to replace is lines ~253-309 (the if/else block for parsing/hasIngredients). Replace the existing <ul> rendering inside ingredientsExpanded with <RecipeIngredientList ... />. Keep the outer toggle button ('X ingredients' chevron) as-is — just swap the expanded content. RecipeHub is at src/components/recipes/RecipeHub.tsx. The onIngredientsChange callback should reload just the affected recipe's ingredients: call supabase.from('recipe_ingredients').select().eq('recipe_id', recipe.id) and update recipeIngredientsMap[recipe.id]. RecipeIngredientList self-manages its data, so the count shown on the toggle button may drift from RecipeHub's recipeIngredientsMap — simplest fix is to let RecipeIngredientList own the count via its internal state and surface it via a prop like onCountChange or just remove the count from the toggle button label (show 'Ingredients' instead of 'X ingredients').

userStories.title: Replace EditRecipeIngredientsDialog in EventRecipesTab with inline RecipeIngredientList
userStories.description: As a user on an Event or Meal detail page, I want to see and edit recipe ingredients inline in the recipe card — same style as My Recipes — instead of opening a dialog.
acceptanceCriteria: EventRecipesTab adds a collapsible 'Ingredients' section below each recipe card header using RecipeIngredientList
A toggle button (e.g. 'X ingredients ▾') shows/hides RecipeIngredientList inline in the card, similar to how notes are toggled
RecipeIngredientList receives editable={recipe.createdBy === userId} so only the owner sees edit controls
EventRecipesTab has new props: userId?: string, onIngredientsChange?: (recipeId: string) => void
The existing 'Ingredients' ListChecks button is removed from EventRecipesTab recipe card headers
The onEditIngredients prop is removed from EventRecipesTabProps
In EventDetailPage: remove editIngredientsRecipe state, EditRecipeIngredientsDialog import and usage, and onEditIngredients prop
In PersonalMealDetailPage: same — remove editIngredientsRecipe state, EditRecipeIngredientsDialog import and usage, and onEditIngredients prop
EventDetailPage passes userId={user?.id} and an onIngredientsChange callback to EventRecipesTab
PersonalMealDetailPage passes userId={user?.id} and an onIngredientsChange callback to EventRecipesTab
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: EventRecipesTab is at src/components/events/EventRecipesTab.tsx (~381 lines). Each recipe card is rendered in a .map() block starting ~line 110. The 'Ingredients' ListChecks button is at lines ~222-233 — remove it. Add a new state Set<string> for expandedIngredients (which recipe ingredient sections are open), add a toggle button in each card similar to the notes toggle (ChevronDown/Up). When expanded, render <RecipeIngredientList recipeId={recipe.id} userId={userId ?? ''} editable={recipe.createdBy === userId} onIngredientsChange={() => onIngredientsChange?.(recipe.id)} />. EventDetailPage usage is around line 964-976 and the dialog is at lines 1376-1390. PersonalMealDetailPage usage is around lines 864-879 and dialog is 882-896. The onIngredientsChange callback in both pages can be a no-op or trigger a light data refresh — since RecipeIngredientList owns its own data, no parent reload is strictly needed.

userStories.title: Delete EditRecipeIngredientsDialog

acceptanceCriteria: src/components/recipes/EditRecipeIngredientsDialog.tsx is deleted
No remaining imports of EditRecipeIngredientsDialog anywhere in the codebase
IngredientFormRows.tsx and ingredientRowTypes.ts are NOT deleted (still used by RecipeInputForm)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: After US-006 and US-007, EditRecipeIngredientsDialog should have zero imports remaining. Verify with grep: grep -r 'EditRecipeIngredientsDialog' src/. IngredientFormRows is used by src/components/recipes/RecipeInputForm.tsx (the recipe creation/editing form) — do not touch it. ingredientRowTypes.ts is used by both IngredientFormRows and RecipeInputForm — do not touch it.

userStories.title: Extract RecipeDetailTabs shared component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared Recipes/Groceries/Pantry tab shell component so that both EventDetailPage and PersonalMealDetailPage use the same tab structure without duplicating markup.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at src/components/shared/RecipeDetailTabs.tsx
Props: recipesContent: React.ReactNode, groceryContent: React.ReactNode, pantryContent: React.ReactNode
Renders a 3-tab pill (Recipes, Groceries, Pantry) with the same icons as EventDetailPage (BookOpen, ShoppingCart, Utensils or equivalent)
Same TabsList className and TabsTrigger styling as the current EventDetailPage tabs (lines ~942-956)
EventDetailPage replaces its inline Tabs/TabsList/TabsContent block with <RecipeDetailTabs recipesContent={...} groceryContent={...} pantryContent={...} />
EventDetailPage UI and behavior is identical to before
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The tabs block to extract from EventDetailPage is lines ~942-1013. It uses Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent from @/components/ui/tabs. The three tab values are 'recipes', 'grocery', 'pantry'. Icons: BookOpen (recipes), ShoppingCart (grocery), Utensils (pantry) — check EventDetailPage imports for exact icon names. The component just renders the shell; callers pass in the content for each tab as ReactNode props. This is a simple extraction — no logic, no state. Create src/components/shared/ directory if it doesn't exist. After extraction, EventDetailPage should render: <RecipeDetailTabs recipesContent={<TabsContent value='recipes'>...</TabsContent>} ... /> — or alternatively, the three *Content nodes can be passed in and RecipeDetailTabs wraps them in TabsContent internally. The latter is cleaner: recipesContent, groceryContent, pantryContent are just the inner JSX, and RecipeDetailTabs wraps each in the appropriate TabsContent.

userStories.title: Add Groceries and Pantry tabs to PersonalMealDetailPage
userStories.description: As a user viewing a personal meal detail, I want Groceries and Pantry tabs (just like the Event detail page) so that I can manage my shopping list and pantry directly from the meal.
acceptanceCriteria: PersonalMealDetailPage imports and initializes useGroceryList with contextType='event' and contextId=event.id (the meal's event ID), userId=user.id
PersonalMealDetailPage uses RecipeDetailTabs to render all three tabs
The Recipes tab contains the existing EventRecipesTab content (unchanged behavior)
The Groceries tab contains GroceryListSection wired to the grocery hook, same props pattern as EventDetailPage
The Pantry tab contains <PantrySection userId={user?.id} onPantryChange={handlePantryChange} />
handlePantryChange in PersonalMealDetailPage calls grocery.handlePantryChange (or equivalent) to update pantry filtering
The grocery list loads the recipes for this meal and shows the combined ingredient list
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PersonalMealDetailPage is at src/pages/PersonalMealDetailPage.tsx (~1132 lines). It already imports EventRecipesTab and renders it directly (lines ~864-879) with no surrounding tabs. To add useGroceryList: import it from @/hooks/useGroceryList. The meal's context is contextType='event', contextId=event.id (the personal meal IS stored as an event in the DB). Check how EventDetailPage calls useGroceryList (around lines 145-200 in EventDetailPage.tsx) and mirror that pattern in PersonalMealDetailPage — pass the event.id as the context. The grocery hook needs recipes (event.recipesWithNotes.map(r => r.recipe)) and userId. GroceryListSection props: mirror what EventDetailPage passes at lines ~980-1007. PantrySection is at src/components/pantry/PantrySection.tsx. For handlePantryChange, EventDetailPage has a pattern around line 200 — copy it. The main layout change: replace the standalone <EventRecipesTab .../> render with <RecipeDetailTabs recipesContent={<EventRecipesTab .../>} groceryContent={<GroceryListSection .../>} pantryContent={<PantrySection .../>} />.

userStories.title: Unit test coverage for new components and hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for all new and modified code from this initiative so that regressions are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/unit/lib/parseIngredientText.test.ts covers: successful parse returns ParsedGroceryItem[], empty text returns [], supabase error throws, edge function skipped returns []

tests/unit/components/recipes/AddIngredientInput.test.tsx covers: renders textarea and Add button, button disabled when textarea empty, calls onSubmit with text on click, shows spinner while submitting, clears textarea after submit

tests/unit/components/recipes/RecipeIngredientList.test.tsx covers: renders grouped ingredients by category, shows loading spinner on mount, edit calls supabase update with new name, delete calls supabase delete, add calls parseIngredientText then supabase insert, empty state renders when no ingredients
tests/unit/hooks/useGroceryList.test.ts is updated to cover handleAddItemsToRecipe: calls parseIngredientText, inserts to recipe_ingredients, calls invalidateCacheAndResetRefs

tests/unit/components/recipes/GroceryListSection.test.tsx is updated to cover: AddIngredientInput renders on per-recipe tabs when onAddItemsToRecipe provided, AddIngredientInput does NOT render on Combined tab
tests/unit/components/recipes/RecipeCard.test.tsx is updated: onEditIngredients prop and ListChecks button no longer exist, RecipeIngredientList renders when ingredients expanded
tests/unit/components/events/EventRecipesTab.test.tsx is updated: onEditIngredients prop removed, RecipeIngredientList renders when ingredient section expanded
tests/unit/components/shared/RecipeDetailTabs.test.tsx covers: renders all three tabs, switching tabs shows correct content
npm run test:run passes with no failures
npm run test:coverage shows 100% coverage on all new files (parseIngredientText.ts, AddIngredientInput.tsx, RecipeIngredientList.tsx, RecipeDetailTabs.tsx)
userStories.notes: This story runs AFTER all implementation stories are complete. Mock patterns to follow: supabase client → vi.mock('@/integrations/supabase/client'), parseIngredientText → vi.mock('@/lib/parseIngredientText'), toast → vi.mock('sonner'). Use createMockUser, createMockIngredient, createMockRecipe from tests/utils.tsx where applicable. For RecipeIngredientList, mock the supabase.from('recipe_ingredients').select() chain to return fixture data. For useGroceryList tests, check existing test file at tests/unit/hooks/ — follow the same vi.resetModules + dynamic import pattern if present. The coverage requirement (100% on new files) means every branch in the new code must be exercised — pay attention to the error/empty/loading branches. Run npm run test:coverage and check the output table for the specific files.

userStories.title: General unit test coverage pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive unit test coverage across the codebase so that regressions in existing code are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Run npm run test:coverage and identify all files below 100% coverage in src/components/, src/hooks/, and src/lib/ (excluding IngredientWheel.tsx which is exempt at ~55%)
Write or extend tests to bring coverage to 100% on all files in src/lib/
Write or extend tests to bring coverage to 100% on all files in src/hooks/
Write or extend tests to bring coverage to 100% on all files in src/components/events/
Write or extend tests to bring coverage to 100% on all files in src/components/ingredients/
Write or extend tests to bring coverage to 100% on all files in src/components/mealplan/
Write or extend tests to bring coverage to 100% on all files in src/components/recipes/
npm run test:run passes with no failures
npm run test:coverage confirms all targeted directories are at 100%
userStories.notes: Run npm run test:coverage first to get the current coverage report — use that output to identify exactly which files/lines/branches are missing coverage rather than guessing. Work file by file, starting with the lowest-hanging fruit (files with highest existing coverage that just need a few more cases). Mock patterns: supabase → vi.mock('@/integrations/supabase/client'), toast → vi.mock('sonner'), use mock factories from tests/utils.tsx (createMockUser, createMockIngredient, createMockRecipe, createMockNote, createMockEvent). IngredientWheel.tsx in src/components/wheel/ is explicitly exempt — do not spend time on it. If a file has coverage gaps only in unreachable defensive code (e.g. a branch that can never be hit due to TypeScript types), it is acceptable to add a /* istanbul ignore next */ comment rather than writing a contorted test. Prioritize branches and lines over statements — branch coverage is the hardest to hit and most valuable. This story may require multiple sub-passes: run coverage, fix gaps, re-run, repeat until all targeted directories are green.
